message:
fix: replace unsafe byte-index string access with boundary-checked alternatives across 6 crates

Several places in the codebase used `as_bytes()[n]` or `as_bytes()[..n]`
to index into strings. While these inputs are typically ASCII, this
pattern is fragile: if a multi-byte UTF-8 character ever appears at
or before the index position, the code would either panic (out of
bounds on the byte array vs char count) or slice mid-codepoint and
produce incorrect comparisons.

Changes by crate:

- cli/output.rs: Replace `line.as_bytes()[col1_start]` with
  `line.bytes().nth(col1_start).unwrap()` in test — semantically
  identical but makes the iterator-based access explicit.

- db/reconstruct.rs: Replace `sanitized.as_bytes()[0].is_ascii_alphanumeric()`
  with `sanitized.chars().next().is_some_and(|c| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric())`
  to safely check the first character without byte indexing.

- server/tui_screens/messages.rs: Two call sites sliced strings via
  `as_bytes()[..prefix_lower.len()]` for case-insensitive comparison.
  Now guarded with `is_char_boundary()` before slicing, and uses
  `str::eq_ignore_ascii_case` on string slices instead of byte slices.

- tools/messaging.rs: Three analogous byte-index patterns replaced —
  `is_valid_thread_id` now uses `chars().next()` instead of
  `as_bytes()[0]`, `sanitize_thread_id` uses the same `chars().next()`
  guard, and `apply_prefix` in tests uses `is_char_boundary()`.

- search-core/two_tier.rs: Replace closure `|r| r.is_empty()` with
  method reference `std::vec::Vec::is_empty` for idiomatic Rust style
  (clippy::redundant_closure lint).

- tools/products.rs: Extract `now_micros as u64` into a named variable
  `time_component` with `#[allow(clippy::cast_sign_loss)]` annotation
  to silence the clippy lint while documenting the intentional cast.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
